bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility by asking for my highest-value option on every issue. The opponent’s opening offer is valid but gives me my lowest value on all issues, and their message is an emotional appeal rather than evidence of concession or constraint.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02) and the opponent has not conceded from their all-valueC opening, which is very low for me. To avoid simply repeating my previous all-valueA offer, I am making a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C. The opponent’s messages are emotional appeals, but their actual offers show no movement yet.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still extremely early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueC package. Their messages are emotional appeals but do not change the numeric signal, so I will make only a small low-weight concession while holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueC offer each time. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the offer history shows no movement, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92, since it is still very early at t=0.08 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held at valueC everywhere but recently moved issueB to valueB, so I will reciprocate modestly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on my most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.883 because it is still early at t=0.10, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only slightly conceded on issueB while otherwise holding mostly at valueC, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding evidence. I will concede on the lower-weight issues D, E, and F first while holding firm on the highest-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has only conceded on issueB from valueC to valueB while holding all other issues at valueC, and their emotional messages do not add binding information beyond that limited concession pattern. I will avoid repeating my last exact bid by conceding further on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.80 because it is early in the negotiation, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent has made only small concessions, shifting one issue at a time from valueC to valueB, while their messages are emotional but not binding. I am holding firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C, while conceding further on lower-weight issues D and E/F to explore a workable tradeoff.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has only made small, inconsistent concessions. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, while their actual offers remain heavily at valueC with only isolated movement on issueB, issueA, or issueD, so I will concede mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am still targeting a high utility around 0.80 because the negotiation is early at t=0.18, so I should not concede on the highest-weight issues A, B, and C yet. The opponent’s offers have only made small, rotating concessions from all valueC to occasional valueB, and their emotional messages are not binding, so I will offer movement mainly on lower-weight issues while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding the lower-weight issues D, E, and F. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I should not over-concede, but the opponent’s latest offer shows a meaningful move on issueB while mostly staying at valueC elsewhere. Their messages are emotional appeals, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.72, conceding meaningfully from my prior position while still protecting my highest-weight issueA and the issueB value the opponent has already shown willingness to offer. At t=0.22 there is still time, so I am not accepting their low-utility latest bid; their messages are emotional appeals, while the actual offers show only limited movement, mainly on issueB and occasional valueB signals on issueC/issueD.

I targeted a utility of about 0.80, which is still appropriate at t=0.24 because there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has started making isolated concessions, especially most recently on issueA, but their full offers are still low-value to me, so I am holding firm on my highest-weight issues while conceding on lower-weight ones.

I am targeting a strong utility bid around 0.76 by holding firm on the three highest-weight issues while conceding the lower-weight issues D/E/F. At t=0.26 there is still time, and the opponent’s offers show only small single-issue concessions rather than a broad move toward my preferences, so I should not drop too far yet despite their cooperative messaging.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72, holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and B while conceding on lower-weight issues and partially on issueC. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I do not need to match the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their history shows some willingness to move on A, B, C, D, and F even if not consistently. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I am relying on the actual bid pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.68 utility, since we are still only at t=0.30 and should not drop near the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has mostly held at valueC but has shown movement on issueB and a few lower-weight issues, while their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding evidence. I will hold firm on the most important issueA and issueC, concede issueB to valueB, and give valueC on the lower-weight issues to improve acceptability.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.604 by holding firm on the most important issueA, accepting the opponent’s movement to valueB on issueB, and asking for a compromise valueB on issueC while conceding the lower-weight issuesD/E/F to valueC. At t=0.32 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest valid offers show real movement on issueA and issueB, so I am making a reciprocal mid-game concession without dropping to their current offer. Their messages are emotional appeals, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65, which is a moderate concession from my ideal while still protecting the most important issue, issueA. Since it is only t=0.34, I do not need to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their bids show real movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF at different points, so I am combining those concessions while giving them valueC on the lower-weight issueD and issueE. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the actual bid history rather than the wording.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier bids but still protects my highest-weight issueA. At t=0.36 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers, though their bids show some movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF; I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and some mid-weight issues while keeping issueA at my best value.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.64, which is a moderate concession from my earlier offers while still preserving value on the highest-weight issueA and issueB. The opponent’s offers remain mostly valueC-heavy, but they have intermittently conceded on issueA, issueB, and issueE, so I am using their latest structure while improving issueA to a value they have already offered before. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual offer history shows only gradual and inconsistent concession, so I should not concede too deeply yet.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.60: a meaningful concession from my earlier stronger offers, but still well above reservation. The opponent’s offers remain mostly low for me, though they have signaled movement on issueA, issueB, and most recently issueE, so I am combining those concessions while holding some value on my most important issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.66, keeping my highest-weight issues A and B at valueA while making concessions on lower-weight issues D/E/F and a moderate concession on issueC. At t=0.42 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to the opponent’s low-utility offers; their bids show intermittent concessions but their latest offer is still only modestly useful to me, so I am responding with a stronger tradeoff. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the actual offer history rather than those claims.

I am targeting a utility around 0.60, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier positions but still protects my higher-weight issues, especially issueA and issueB. At t=0.44 there is moderate time pressure, so I am combining concessions the opponent has actually signaled across prior offers rather than relying on their emotional messages. The opponent has shown willingness at different times to move on issueA, issueB, and most recently issueD, while still mostly holding issueC/issueE/issueF low, so this bid reflects that pattern.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.60 while preserving my most important issue, issueA, at valueA. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer history: they have shown some willingness to move on issueB, issueD, and issueF, while still mostly holding issueA/issueC/issueE low. This bid matches much of their latest structure but keeps issueA at my top value to avoid conceding too much before the deadline.

I am targeting a mid-game compromise around 0.65 utility, still protecting high-weight issueA while conceding mostly on lower-weight issueE/issueF and accepting some middle values on issueC/issueD that the opponent has recently signaled. At t=0.48 there is some pressure to move toward their demonstrated offers, but not enough to drop to their latest 0.563-utility bid for me. Their messages are cooperative but repetitive cheap talk, so I rely mainly on the offer history, especially their recent willingness to give issueA=valueA and issueC/issueD=valueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72, which is a meaningful mid-game concession while still protecting my highest-weight issueA and strong value on issueB. The opponent’s offers have improved from all valueC to mixed concessions, with the latest giving me valueB/valueA/valueB on some issues, but their emotional messages are cheap talk and I rely on the bid history. Since we are only at t=0.50, I will not drop to their exact offer yet, but I will incorporate several concessions they have signaled.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.68, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueE/issueF while still protecting the high-weight issueA and some value on issueB/issueC. The opponent’s offers have improved from all valueC to mixed bids including concessions on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, though their emotional messages are cheap talk and I rely on the bid history instead. Since we are just past halfway, I will make a meaningful but not final concession using values the opponent has already shown willingness to include.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.69, keeping firm on the highest-weight issueA while making concessions on lower-weight issues D/E/F and using valueB compromises on B/C. The opponent has gradually moved from all valueC toward valueB/valueA on some issues, but their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer pattern rather than their claims. Since time is just past halfway, I am not dropping to their latest bid yet, but I am making a materially more acceptable counter than my earlier high-utility positions.

I am targeting roughly 0.68 utility: a small concession from my prior bid while still protecting my highest-weight issue, issueA. At t=0.56 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to accept their latest low-utility offer; their bids show some movement, especially on issueB and occasionally issueA, while the emotional messages are cheap talk rather than binding constraints.

I am targeting about 0.68 utility, since we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline, so I should make a serious tradeoff without dropping to the opponent’s latest low-utility offer. The opponent’s offers have improved over time and their latest bid already gives me issueC=valueB and issueE=valueA, while they still push hard for valueC on several issues; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and move toward compromise on issueB while conceding low-weight issueD and issueF.

I am targeting about 0.68 utility by holding firm on my most important issueA while accepting the opponent’s latest positions on issueB through issueF. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, so I am making a focused concession package close to their last valid offer; their messages are emotional cheap talk, but their bids show willingness to give valueA on issueB and issueF and occasional movement on issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72: meaningful concession from ideal, but still protecting the high-weight issues B and C while allowing movement on issueA and low-weight E/F. At t=0.62 there is some time pressure, but it is not yet the deadline, so I am combining concessions the opponent has already signaled across prior valid offers rather than accepting their latest low-utility package. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer history, which shows gradual movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.65: below my earlier bids but still safely above reservation, reflecting that we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent’s latest valid offer already gives me valueB on issueA and valueA on issueC, but keeps issueB at valueC; since issueB is important to me and they have previously offered valueB/valueA there, I am asking for issueB: valueB while largely matching their latest concessions elsewhere. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer history rather than the wording.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69, conceding on lower-weight issueD while holding stronger positions on issueC and partial concessions on issueA/issueB. At t=0.66 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest lower-utility offer; their history shows they have previously offered issueC:valueA and issueA:valueB, while their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than binding constraints.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72: still above the opponent’s latest offer to me, but with meaningful concessions on lower-weight issues as time is now past the midpoint. The opponent’s bids show real concessions on issueA and issueC, including their latest offer, while their emotional messages are cheap talk; I will hold A/C strongly, ask for a moderate improvement on issueB, and concede issueE/issueF plus some of issueD.
